output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, state deadlines, and how to send a demand letter in 60 seconds for only $19.99.
content_outline: Why Is Your Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit?
State Deadlines: How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return Your Deposit?
What Can a Landlord Legally Deduct? (And What They Can't)
How to Write a Security Deposit Demand Letter That Actually Works
Escalating to Small Claims Court: When and How
Get Your Money Back in 60 Seconds Without a Lawyer
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days and provide an itemized list of any deductions
Landlords cannot legally deduct for normal wear and tear or routine cleaning; only damage beyond ordinary use qualifies
A formal, state-specific demand letter is often the fastest and most affordable way to recover a withheld deposit before going to court
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your official security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com for just $19.99 — no signup, no subscription, and it takes less than 60 seconds.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and need a fast, affordable way to recover their money
